title: Arla Fett

'''Arla Fett''' was the older sister of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], and the only daughter of their [[Fett (Elder Journeyman Protector)|Journeyman Protector father]]. She was also the long-lost aunt of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]].

==Biography==
Born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], Arla spent her youth living on the [[Fett homestead]] with her parents and brother, Jango. In [[58 BBY/Legends|58 BBY]], when she was 14,<ref name="501st" /> the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Civil War]]—fought between the [[True Mandalorians]], under [[Jaster Mereel]], and the splinter group known as the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]], under [[Tor Vizsla]]—spread to Concord Dawn. When the Death Watch began hunting down members of the True Mandalorians that escaped from a lost battle, Arla's father offered Mereel and his men refuge on their farm. The Death Watch tracked them to the homestead and killed her father as her brother Jango was fleeing. When they turned their attention on Jango, Arla witnessed their mother shoot and kill one of the Death Watch members with a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], only to be killed herself. While Jango fled and would be later be rescued by the Mandalorians, Vizsla ordered there be no witnesses and Arla was to be "taken care of".<ref name="JFOS" />

Thus, Arla was believed to have been killed on Concord Dawn along with her parents. Seemingly unknown to anyone else, she was spared by the members of the Death Watch, and was held hostage by them. They viewed her as a prize, and branded their logo onto her back. After a while as their prisoner, Arla joined the Death Watch cause as an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]], and only left them when she got arrested for a shooting [[32 BBY/Legends|10 years before the end]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. While in custody, Arla was deemed mentally unstable and was ordered to be held in the [[Valorum Center]]—a mental institution on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]—for many years.<ref name="501st" /><ref name="O66ARCN" />

In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], three years after Jango's [[Death/Legends|death]] on at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of]] [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]-turned-Mandalorian, [[Bardan Jusik]], visited the center looking for [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]], Dr. [[Ovolot Qail Uthan]]. At the time of his first visit, Jusik had walked past Arla's cell, hearing words that resembled [[Mando'a/Legends|the Mandalorian language]].<ref name="O66ARCN" /> Later, during the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Separatist invasion of Coruscant]], [[Kal Skirata]] and Jusik returned to the center to free Uthan from her cell and upon doing so, Bardan again heard words that resembled Mando'a. The ex-Jedi acted on impulse and decided to free the prisoner, and when the group was picked up by [[Walon Vau]] afterward by a [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]] gunship, Jusik revealed her to be Arla Fett, Jango's lost and supposedly dead sister. Arla would go to [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] along with Jusik and the rest of the Skirata's rogue band when they fled Coruscant following the issue of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], taking up residence in an old storage room at their bastion home of [[Kyrimorut/Legends|Kyrimorut]].<ref name="O66ARCN" />

At Kyrimorut, attempts were made to regulate Arla's medication from the regimen given at Valorum Center, but the dosages were insufficient and Arla attacked Jusik without any provocation. After hearing Arla's story and her insistence that she forgot everything, Jusik offered to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to wipe her memory of the traumatic events in exchange for intel on the Death Watch.<ref name="501st" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|After I was kidnapped, after it stopped being a nightmare, I stayed with them. I became one of them. I ''stayed''. I could have run away, but I ''stayed''. Could you stand being me?|Arla Fett, to Bardan Jusik|<ref name="501st" />}}
Arla Fett, after her capture by the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]], was resentful of her captors, but grew to care for them. Instead of attempting to escape them, she joined their cause as an assassin. Through Death Watch intel, she found out that her younger brother Jango had survived, but made no move to reunite with him. During her arrest and incarceration at the [[Valorum Center]], she was heavily sedated in an effort to prevent her from killing herself over the guilt she felt in her willing participation in a group that killed her family. After she was rescued by [[Bardan Jusik]] and brought back to [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], her medication was changed, and while she was more lucid, her memories and the guilt came flooding back, and she ended up launching an unprovoked attack at Jusik while wandering around the compound. Later on, in a moment of clarity, Arla begged Jusik to kill her in an attempt to put her out of her misery, but he refused. Instead, the pair agreed to a compromise that involved Jusik erasing her memories in exchange for intel on the Death Watch.<ref name="501st" />

Arla spoke the language of Concord Dawn, Concordian, which has loan words from Mando'a.<ref name="O66ARCN" /> By her own admission, Arla was a "good shot" with a blaster, which helped her in her career as a Death Watch assassin.<ref name="501st" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the unreleased ''[[Imperial Commando 2]]'' Bardan Jusik would have rubbed Arla Fett's memory, enabling her to cope with her trauma. Afterwards, she would have forgotten being Arla Fett or what had happened to her family, but she didn't feel the pain of memory. Arla would later go on to marry a young man, Bardan Jusik.<ref name="FAQ">{{WebCite|url=http://www.karentraviss.com/page22/files/How_would_you_have_wrapped_u101.html|text=How would you have wrapped up Imperial Commando #2 if you hadn't pulled out of it?|author=Karen Traviss|archivedate=20150420003759}}</ref> In ''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'', Jusik mentions [[Bardan Jusik's wife|his wife]] but it was not canonically confirmed if this wife was Arla.<ref name="LOTF Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>

Arla Fett first appeared in the comic [[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']], but as an unnamed sister in a flashback. Most of her backstory, including her name, was fleshed out by [[Karen Traviss]] in the novels ''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' and ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''.

The character of Arla Fett suffered from an in-universe version of [[Wikipedia:Stockholm syndrome|Stockholm syndrome]], a proposed condition where a captive comes to care for, and even love, her captors.

==Appearances==
